The AiFeedbackButtons in streaming mode receive an empty-string suggestionId. This means feedback submissions will fail server-side (no valid suggestion ID). A follow-up step should either hide feedback buttons in streaming mode or wire up the suggestionId once the backend returns it.

confidenceTier is computed from the raw `matches` list (pre-rerank), not from the reranked results. This means a query that reranks down from 10 high-score matches to 3 low-score ones will still report HIGH confidence. This is technically correct per spec but could be misleading in edge cases — worth revisiting in a future step if product feedback indicates over-reporting of confidence.

Tests don't stub `calibrationService.getCalibration()`, so Mockito will return null by default. The service gracefully handles this (catches exception, skips calibration), but tests could be more explicit about the two paths (with vs without calibration).

The multi-hop path uses the same intent/filter for all sub-queries. For highly divergent compound questions (e.g., "Alice's commits and also Bob's overall plan"), a single intent classification may miss entity-type or user filters specific to each sub-query. Addressed by design (single intent is simpler and sufficient for most cases), but worth noting for future refinement.
Pre-existing retry loop double-counts rate-limit tokens on parse failure (noted in steps 1-9, out of scope here)

confidenceTier is computed from pre-rerank `matches` in the original code but is now correctly from reranked results. The prior observation about this is resolved in the current code (toConfidenceMatches converts reranked → confidence matches using rerankScore).

Multi-hop retrieval uses one shared intent/filter derived from the full rewritten question, not per-sub-query intent classification. This is acceptable for this step but may limit precision for compound queries with divergent filters.

`getRecommendations()` returns `whatIfResult = null` for all persisted rows. The frontend (Step 13) must handle null whatIfResult gracefully, and may want to call the refresh endpoint to get populated simulation data on first load.

BLOCKED_CRITICAL and SCOPE_VOLATILITY signals produce partial recommendations (no WhatIfResponse simulation) with only descriptive suggestedAction text. This is by design since there's no natural commit mutation to simulate for these signal types.
